# On exit paths

Another signature move from the same essayist:

"Before you commit, walk the exit path. What does it look like to stop?
If there is no way to stop cleanly, you are not making a decision, you
are setting a trap for your future self."

Example case: the essayist refused a funding round in year 3 because the
term sheet had no clean exit — they described a similar trap they had
watched two other founders fall into.

"Optionality is the asset. Every irreversible commitment without an exit
is optionality you sold for cash. Measure the cash against the optionality
before signing."
